definitions.name: inheritanceDependenceRate
definitions.definition: Stabilized inheritance dependence signal combining run-level dependence incidence with scenario-implied fragility from inheritance removal stress tests.
definitions.calculationNote: Observed signal blends run-level incidence (0.022) with scenario-implied fragility (0.7391).

sensitivityValidation.note: Observed and scenario-implied dependence diverge by 0.2151; export includes reconciledDependenceRate to make this gap explicit.

definitions.name: homeSaleDependenceRate
definitions.definition: Stabilized home-sale dependence signal combining run-level dependence incidence with scenario-implied fragility from home-sale removal stress tests.
definitions.calculationNote: Observed signal blends run-level incidence (0.15) with scenario-implied fragility (0.2471).

sensitivityValidation.note: Stabilized observed dependence is directionally aligned with home-sale removal sensitivity.

inferredAssumptions: Ambiguous holding TRP_2030: Target-date 2030 mapped using configured account-level assumption.
Ambiguous holding CENTRAL_MANAGED: Managed sleeve mapped using configured account-level assumption.
Contribution settings patch uses annualized payroll targets; in-year effect is prorated by salary timing.
Social Security flow assumes full-year benefits once modeled age reaches claim age.

recommendations.title: Protect ACA bridge eligibility
recommendations.action: In 2027, keep MAGI near or below $84,969 by shifting withdrawals away from ordinary-income sources where possible.
recommendations.triggerReason: ACA bridge year is currently modeled above subsidy-friendly MAGI limits.
tradeoffs: Could require lower near-term discretionary spending or altered withdrawal sourcing.
Can preserve subsidy support in bridge years before Medicare.

confidence.label: high
confidence.rationale: Strong effect, stable across sensitivities (5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.), and faithful model inputs.

sensitivityConsistency.rationale: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.

notes: Impact estimated via seeded planner-enhanced path counterfactual.
Sensitivity stability: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.
Model completeness: faithful. All required core inputs and assumptions are explicitly provided.
Ranking: netBenefit=1.106 category=aca objective=maximize_time_weighted_spending.

recommendations.title: Align spending to supported level
recommendations.action: Trim monthly spending by about $343 to match today's supported level.
recommendations.triggerReason: Target spending currently exceeds modeled supported spending at the active guardrail settings.
tradeoffs: Immediate lifestyle flexibility may decrease in discretionary categories.
Can improve guardrail headroom in downside paths.

confidence.label: high
confidence.rationale: Strong effect, stable across sensitivities (5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.), and faithful model inputs.

sensitivityConsistency.rationale: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.

notes: Impact estimated via seeded planner-enhanced path counterfactual.

Ranking: netBenefit=0.757 category=spending objective=maximize_time_weighted_spending.

recommendations.title: Balance withdrawal source concentration
recommendations.action: Current route leans heavily on cash (~100% of 2026 withdrawals). Keep source mix diversified to manage taxes and guardrail cliffs.
recommendations.triggerReason: Near-term withdrawal sourcing is concentrated in one bucket, reducing flexibility under changing conditions.
tradeoffs: Smoothing sources can increase planning complexity year-to-year.
Can reduce threshold-cliff risk and improve optionality later.

confidence.label: medium
confidence.rationale: Moderate net signal from effect + sensitivity stability (5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.); model completeness is faithful.

narrative.whereThingsStand: You are tracking about $916,923 net worth, salary through 2027-07-01, 2 modeled windfall(s) totaling $1,000,000 starting in 2028, and estimated Social Security of about $73,200/year once claims start. The latest Monte Carlo read is 95.8% success.
narrative.whatMattersNow: Highest priority right now: align monthly spending with supported level and protect aca bridge subsidy eligibility. The top actions are sorted by urgency and quantified gap size so execution can focus on the most material near-term moves first.

actionCards.title: Align monthly spending with supported level
actionCards.detail: Target spending is about $343/month above the currently supported path.
actionCards.whyItMatters: Closing this gap is usually the fastest lever to improve success odds without changing retirement timing.

actionCards.title: Protect ACA bridge subsidy eligibility
actionCards.detail: Bridge year 2027 MAGI is about $2,622 over the modeled ACA-friendly ceiling ($84,969).
actionCards.whyItMatters: Managing MAGI in bridge years protects premium support and prevents avoidable healthcare-cost spikes.

actionCards.title: Protect ACA bridge eligibility
actionCards.detail: In 2027, keep MAGI near or below $84,969 by shifting withdrawals away from ordinary-income sources where possible.
actionCards.whyItMatters: ACA bridge year is currently modeled above subsidy-friendly MAGI limits.

phases.label: Pre-Retirement
phases.objective: Build runway and execution readiness before earned income stops.

phases.label: Bridge To Medicare
phases.objective: Manage MAGI and withdrawal sourcing while ACA subsidy sensitivity is highest.

actions.title: ACA bridge payroll MAGI reducer
actions.objective: Use paycheck deferrals first (pre-tax 401(k) and HSA) to reduce bridge-year MAGI before portfolio trade moves.
actions.whyNow: Bridge-year MAGI in 2027 is modeled above the ACA-friendly ceiling by $2,622 and salary is still active.

laymanExpansion.storyHook: This is a bridge move so healthcare income rules do not force bad timing decisions.
laymanExpansion.plainEnglishTask: Update payroll settings: pre-tax 401(k) target to $36,723/yr, Roth 401(k) target to $0/yr, HSA target to $3,339/yr.
laymanExpansion.whyImportant: This reduces MAGI pressure before retirement withdrawals start. Base scenario impact is +0.0 pts success and +$0 per month of supported spending, with 100% direction consistency in sensitivity runs.
walkthroughSteps: Do this in the next 30 days.
Open payroll elections and set pre-tax 401(k), Roth 401(k), and HSA to the target values shown.
If ACA headroom is tight, favor pre-tax + HSA first and move Roth deferrals only if needed.
After payroll changes are in place, rerun the plan and verify runway and ACA headroom moved in the intended direction.
watchOuts: Payroll election changes can take one or two pay cycles to show up; timing matters late in the year.
Do not exceed annual IRS limits across pre-tax + Roth employee 401(k) deferrals combined.
HSA contributions require HSA eligibility; keep coverage type and eligibility current.

inferredAssumptions: Contribution settings patch uses annualized payroll targets; in-year effect is prorated by salary timing.

actions.title: Conservative ACA bridge cash staging
actions.objective: Pre-fund bridge-year spending from taxable liquidity so MAGI management has more room.
actions.whyNow: Bridge-year MAGI in 2027 is modeled above the ACA-friendly ceiling.

laymanExpansion.storyHook: This is a bridge move so healthcare income rules do not force bad timing decisions.
laymanExpansion.plainEnglishTask: Move about $2,903 from CENTRAL_MANAGED into CASH across your taxable accounts.
laymanExpansion.whyImportant: Base scenario impact is +0.0 pts success and +$0 per month of supported spending, with 100% direction consistency in sensitivity runs.
walkthroughSteps: Do this in the next 30 days.
Step 1: In Joint WROS - TOD (Managed), move about $2,903 (3.5% of CENTRAL_MANAGED) from CENTRAL_MANAGED to CASH.
After the trades settle, run the plan again and compare success rate, supported spending, and annual federal tax.
watchOuts: Taxable-account moves can realize capital gains; check tax lot impact before trading.
Use these dollar amounts as guides; if prices move, keep the direction and approximate size.
Pause and re-check if this conflicts with near-term cash needs, withdrawals, or required distributions.

phases.label: Medicare + IRMAA Shaping
phases.objective: Stay ahead of two-year IRMAA lookback pressure with smoother income timing.

actions.title: Conservative IRMAA lookback flexibility buffer
actions.objective: Build flexibility before IRMAA lookback years so withdrawals can stay under surcharge cliffs when needed.
actions.whyNow: Modeled IRMAA pressure begins in 2037, which maps to tax year 2035 lookback.

laymanExpansion.storyHook: This creates room so IRMAA lookback years are less likely to surprise your premiums.

trustPanel.summary: Run quality needs attention before treating recommendations as execution-ready.

checks.title: Data fidelity and explicit assumptions
checks.detail: Model completeness is faithful with no inferred assumptions.

checks.title: Home-sale modeling completeness
checks.detail: Home-sale tax basis and net liquidity are explicitly modeled.

checks.title: Inheritance treatment explicitness
checks.detail: Inheritance tax treatment is explicitly modeled as "cash_non_taxable".

checks.title: Recommendations backed by measured deltas
checks.detail: 100% of top recommendations exceed minimum measured impact thresholds.

checks.title: Explicit Roth conversion policy
checks.detail: Roth conversion policy is explicitly configured in model rules.

checks.title: Closed-loop withdrawal logic
checks.detail: Closed-loop healthcare/tax solving enabled with up to 10 passes; average yearly convergence 99%.

checks.title: Inheritance dependency risk
checks.detail: Dependence rate 66%; remove-inheritance success delta -68.0 pts.

checks.title: Home-sale dependency risk
checks.detail: Dependence rate 22%; remove-home-sale success delta -21.0 pts.

checks.title: Phased spending realism
checks.detail: Time-weighted objective active; spending phase spread 0.0%.

strategicPrep.title: Protect ACA bridge eligibility
strategicPrep.action: In 2027, keep MAGI near or below $84,969 by shifting withdrawals away from ordinary-income sources where possible.
strategicPrep.triggerReason: ACA bridge year is currently modeled above subsidy-friendly MAGI limits.
tradeoffs: Could require lower near-term discretionary spending or altered withdrawal sourcing.
Can preserve subsidy support in bridge years before Medicare.

confidence.label: high
confidence.rationale: Strong effect, stable across sensitivities (5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.), and faithful model inputs.

sensitivityConsistency.rationale: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.

notes: Impact estimated via seeded planner-enhanced path counterfactual.
Sensitivity stability: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.
Model completeness: faithful. All required core inputs and assumptions are explicitly provided.
Ranking: netBenefit=1.106 category=aca objective=maximize_time_weighted_spending.

strategicPrep.title: Align spending to supported level
strategicPrep.action: Trim monthly spending by about $343 to match today's supported level.
strategicPrep.triggerReason: Target spending currently exceeds modeled supported spending at the active guardrail settings.
tradeoffs: Immediate lifestyle flexibility may decrease in discretionary categories.
Can improve guardrail headroom in downside paths.

confidence.label: high
confidence.rationale: Strong effect, stable across sensitivities (5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.), and faithful model inputs.

sensitivityConsistency.rationale: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.

notes: Impact estimated via seeded planner-enhanced path counterfactual.
Sensitivity stability: 5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.

Ranking: netBenefit=0.757 category=spending objective=maximize_time_weighted_spending.

strategicPrep.title: Balance withdrawal source concentration
strategicPrep.action: Current route leans heavily on cash (~100% of 2026 withdrawals). Keep source mix diversified to manage taxes and guardrail cliffs.
strategicPrep.triggerReason: Near-term withdrawal sourcing is concentrated in one bucket, reducing flexibility under changing conditions.
tradeoffs: Smoothing sources can increase planning complexity year-to-year.
Can reduce threshold-cliff risk and improve optionality later.

confidence.label: medium
confidence.rationale: Moderate net signal from effect + sensitivity stability (5/5 sensitivity scenarios kept the same impact direction.); model completeness is faithful.

action.title: ACA bridge payroll MAGI reducer
action.objective: Use paycheck deferrals first (pre-tax 401(k) and HSA) to reduce bridge-year MAGI before portfolio trade moves.
action.whyNow: Bridge-year MAGI in 2027 is modeled above the ACA-friendly ceiling by $2,622 and salary is still active.

laymanExpansion.storyHook: This is a bridge move so healthcare income rules do not force bad timing decisions.
laymanExpansion.plainEnglishTask: Update payroll settings: pre-tax 401(k) target to $36,723/yr, Roth 401(k) target to $0/yr, HSA target to $3,339/yr.
laymanExpansion.whyImportant: This reduces MAGI pressure before retirement withdrawals start. Base scenario impact is +0.0 pts success and +$0 per month of supported spending, with 100% direction consistency in sensitivity runs.
walkthroughSteps: Do this in the next 30 days.
Open payroll elections and set pre-tax 401(k), Roth 401(k), and HSA to the target values shown.
If ACA headroom is tight, favor pre-tax + HSA first and move Roth deferrals only if needed.
After payroll changes are in place, rerun the plan and verify runway and ACA headroom moved in the intended direction.
watchOuts: Payroll election changes can take one or two pay cycles to show up; timing matters late in the year.
Do not exceed annual IRS limits across pre-tax + Roth employee 401(k) deferrals combined.
HSA contributions require HSA eligibility; keep coverage type and eligibility current.

inferredAssumptions: Contribution settings patch uses annualized payroll targets; in-year effect is prorated by salary timing.

laymanExpansion.whyImportant: Base scenario impact is +0.0 pts success and +$0 per month of supported spending, with 100% direction consistency in sensitivity runs.
walkthroughSteps: Queue this for the next quarter.
Step 1: In Rollover IRA, move about $13,129 (7.2% of SCHD) from SCHD to CASH.
After the trades settle, run the plan again and compare success rate, supported spending, and annual federal tax.
watchOuts: Keep this inside the same tax bucket to avoid accidental tax consequences.
Use these dollar amounts as guides; if prices move, keep the direction and approximate size.
Pause and re-check if this conflicts with near-term cash needs, withdrawals, or required distributions.

topSuppressionReasons.reason: Filtered by evidence gate: confidence score is below policy minimum.
Filtered by evidence gate: tax-only signal requires explicit policy allowlist or stronger net economic benefit.
Filtered by evidence gate: measured deltas are below minimum signal thresholds (success/spend/funded/wealth/tax).

items.title: Roth conversion sizing to RMD start
items.summary: Need roughly $31,399/year of pre-tax reduction between retirement (2027) and RMD start (2039) to fully drain today's pre-tax balance. Use as sizing anchor, not final recommendation.

items.title: Home sale capital-gains treatment
items.summary: Estimated taxable gain from home sale is $0 after exclusion.

items.title: Home sale liquidity assumption
items.summary: Home sale liquidity is explicitly modeled at $460,000 (gross sale amount $500,000).

items.title: Social Security taxation
items.summary: Federal tax engine includes provisional-income Social Security taxation each year.

items.title: Healthcare inflation trajectory
items.summary: Healthcare premiums are projected with 5.5% annual medical inflation.

items.title: Early-retirement runway exposure
items.summary: Cash runway is short by $56,592 vs 18-month essential-plus-fixed target ($108,000).

items.title: HSA spend-down strategy
items.summary: HSA strategy is enabled and available for healthcare funding decisions.

items.title: Long-term care tail risk
items.summary: Long-term care cost assumption is active with 45% event probability.

modelFidelity.effectOnReliability: High reliability: key assumptions are explicit and mostly exact.

checks.label: Strategic recommendations include full evidence packets (baseline/counterfactual + MAGI/downside deltas)
checks.detail: All returned strategic recommendations include deterministic snapshots, MAGI/downside deltas, confidence, and sensitivity consistency.

checks.label: Accepted recommendation IDs match returned recommendation set
checks.detail: Recommendation ID ledger is internally consistent.

checks.label: Recommendation suppression/acceptance counts are explicitly disclosed
checks.detail: Returned 3 recommendations with 0 pre-evaluation skips, 0 hard-constraint suppressions, 3 evidence-gated suppressions, and 0 ranking suppressions.

checks.label: Canonical scorecard metrics are internally consistent across export sections
checks.detail: Canonical success/completeness/dependence metrics are aligned across summary, trust, and diagnostics sections.

checks.label: Export includes required scenario sensitivity matrices
checks.detail: Inheritance and objective calibration scenario diagnostics are present.

checks.label: Dependence metrics include explicit definitions and scenario-sensitivity reconciliation
checks.detail: Inheritance/home-sale dependence rates include definition, calculation note, and sensitivity consistency metadata.

checks.label: Runway risk model reports downside deltas and risk-reduction score
checks.detail: Runway risk score 100.00 (provenBenefit=true).

checks.label: Runway recommendations only surface when downside-risk evidence proves benefit
checks.detail: No runway recommendation returned without proven runway downside-risk benefit.

checks.label: Model completeness indicator is explicitly declared
checks.detail: Model completeness is reconstructed; inferred assumptions are explicitly surfaced.

checks.label: Model fidelity layer classifies input quality and reliability impact
checks.detail: Fidelity score 89.47 with 0 blocking and 4 soft assumptions.

checks.label: Blocking assumptions are explicitly surfaced
checks.detail: No blocking assumptions detected by fidelity checks.

checks.label: Model trust section is explicit (trust level, score, assumptions, and input breakdown)
checks.detail: Trust level planning_grade with fidelity score 89.47.

checks.label: Reconstructed models include an explicit checklist to reach faithful fidelity
checks.detail: Checklist includes 4 input upgrades to reach faithful fidelity.

checks.label: Inheritance dependence headline is explicit in export
checks.detail: Plan is inheritance-dependent; robustness 56.52 with fragility penalty 43.48.

checks.label: Downside metrics exported for raw and planner-enhanced outcomes
checks.detail: Early failure, shortfall severity, worst-decile wealth, and adverse equity-sale rates are exported.

checks.label: Closed-loop convergence diagnostics exported for both simulation modes
checks.detail: Both raw and planner-enhanced outcomes include converged status, passes used, stop reason, and final deltas.

checks.label: No-change closed-loop stop reason does not overstate threshold convergence
checks.detail: No-change convergence is only marked converged when deltas remain within configured thresholds.

checks.label: Roth conversion trace includes explicit yearly reason codes and summary counts
checks.detail: Planner-enhanced diagnostics include yearly Roth decision reasons and aggregated reason counts.

checks.label: Run-level closed-loop convergence proof is exported for every simulation trial
checks.detail: Run-level convergence entries match simulation runs (raw 500/500, planner 500/500).
